name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 52 Sunday. €41.25/MWh Friday close (TradingEconomics/OilPriceAPI), -1.96% day, -26% month from €56 April 13 peak. Friday ceasefire-optimism deflated price (drivers: ceasefire-extension pricing + weak Asian demand + Cheniere ramp + Qatar Ras Laffan timeline unchanged). Sunday SNSC re-pricing (civilian-apex ratification of IRGC closure + $1/bbl fees) + CONFIRMED Ras Laffan 3-5yr recovery (QatarEnergy CEO Al-Kaabi March 20) = structural floor likely re-establishes €45-50 Monday. TTF still well above pre-war €20-30 range. European gas storage ~30% (5-year seasonal low). If Iran re-closure event fires (Baghaei 'reciprocal measures' + SNSC enforcement), TTF snaps back to €48-52 within a week. Deutsche Welle (Germany) running 'Europe gas security' frame on Ras Laffan partial-restart timelines — German industrial press the most alarmist European voice, consistent with Germany's structural energy vulnerability. ECB next meeting April 29-30. FT/Economist restraint Sunday means editorial catch-up ahead if Monday gaps hard.

signals.content: TTF at 54.52 euros/MWh — doubled from pre-crisis ~25 euros but still well below 2022 peak of ~350. Primary drivers: Ras Laffan repair timeline slipping (Reuters reports Q3 vs Q2), Hormuz toll booth reducing LNG tanker transits, and Houthi entry raising Red Sea risk for LNG carriers. European gas storage at ~35% (adequate for spring but problematic if refill is disrupted for summer). (Sources: OilPriceAPI, ICE Endex, Reuters)

TTF surged 70% in March (EUR38->EUR54.52) — worst monthly gain since Sep 2021. European gas storage at 30% capacity after harsh winter (historically low). Four countries enacted emergency energy measures: Spain cut energy VAT from 21% to 10%, Italy allocated EUR100M for industrial support, Slovakia and Slovenia introduced pump rationing, France removed winter diesel requirements. ECB postponed planned rate cut March 19, raised inflation forecast, cut GDP growth projection — stagflation trap confirmed alongside Fed and RBI. Atlantic Council published 'How the Iran war could trigger a European energy crisis.' Bloomberg: 'Energy Price Shock Is Getting Very Real in Europe.' (Sources: Euronews, IRU, Atlantic Council, Bloomberg, OilPriceAPI)

TTF pulled back slightly to EUR54.18/MWh (-1.9%) tracking Brent de-escalation hopes after diplomacy signals. Slight pullback but underlying drivers unchanged: Ras Laffan structural damage (5-year repair), Hormuz toll system reducing LNG tanker transits, European storage at 30% (critically low heading into refill season). If blockade persists through summer, EUR80-100/MWh still realistic. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, ICE Endex)

Eurostat flash estimate: Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% (from 1.9% in Feb), with energy component swinging from -3.1% to +4.9% — an 8 percentage point reversal in one month, the most dramatic since 2022 Russia-Ukraine shock. Shell warned Europe could face 'shortage of fuel as early as April.' TTF at EUR53.73/MWh (slightly cooled from EUR55.80 but +72% from pre-crisis). EU gas storage at ~30% — lowest seasonal level since before 2022. Chemical and steel manufacturers imposing surcharges up to 30%. 'Permanent deindustrialization' language appearing in European business media. BASF raised prices 30%. Huntsman added EUR200/mt gas surcharge. After IEA's 400M barrel SPR release, only Japan (124 days) meets 90-day reserve requirement; Germany 76 days, France 70, UK 39, Australia 27. (Sources: Eurostat, Shell, CNBC, Euronews, Atlantic Council, IEA)

TTF at EUR 47.51/MWh, easing on de-escalation hopes (-6.4% daily). Goldman forecasts EUR 55 for April. EU gas storage at 30% — a five-year seasonal low. To meet winter targets, EU needs to inject 60 bcm by December, which is structurally impossible without Hormuz LNG supplies resuming. Shell's April fuel shortage warning has arrived: 'we are now IN the window.' Slovenia rationing. Spain spending EUR 5B on emergency energy measures. 1.2M bpd of NW European refining capacity offline for scheduled maintenance, compounding the crisis at the worst possible time. CONFIRMED. (Sources: TradingEconomics, ICE Endex, Goldman, Shell)

Heating oil +3.7% vs gasoline -7.3% SPLIT signal — the distillate crisis is structural even if headline crude normalizes. Approximately 10% of global gasoil and ~20% of jet fuel transits Hormuz directly (Kpler data). Europe's energy crisis is deepening independent of headline oil prices because middle distillates (diesel, heating oil, jet fuel) face specific supply constraints that crude oil easing does not resolve. This divergence signals a structural refining/supply chain problem, not just a commodity price issue. CONFIRMED. (Sources: Kpler, EIA)

TTF eased to EUR49.95/MWh (from EUR55.80 peak March 30) — still +60% from pre-crisis. Easing driven by de-escalation hopes on April 1 (Pezeshkian peace signal) but structural supply deficit unchanged — Qatar LNG remains offline, Ras Laffan 5-year repair. ECB projects inflation surging to 3.1% Q2 driven by energy. EU gas storage at ~30% — lowest seasonal level. Shell: fuel shortages 'as early as April.' ECB rate hike probability at 84%. The TTF-to-ECB feedback loop at maximum weight (10.0 both directions) — gas prices drive inflation expectations which drive ECB hawkishness which constrains fiscal response. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, CNBC, Euronews, ECB)

TTF at EUR 49.98/MWh (April 5). Down -0.6% from April 4 EUR 50.43. Still 60%+ above pre-crisis EUR 31. Was +50% weekly jump per Trading News. Qatar LNG offline. ECB raised inflation forecast to 2.6%. TTF gas is the transmission mechanism for ECB policy — if it re-tests EUR 55+, rate hike becomes near-certain. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, ICE Endex, Trading News)

TTF at EUR49.95/MWh, estimated +3-5% 1D. March range EUR43.30-61.85 (avg EUR53.08). Still 60%+ above pre-crisis EUR31.20. Qatar LNG shutdown + Hormuz shipping risk maintaining elevated prices. ECB revised 2026 inflation forecast UP to 2.6% due to energy costs, growth forecast DOWN to 0.9%. Eurozone March inflation jumped to 2.5% with energy component +4.9%. Rate hike speculation emerging for first time since 2023 — ECB in same stagflation trap as Fed. TTF directly feeds European fertilizer production costs via Haber-Bosch process (BASF +10.4% 1M). (Sources: ICE Endex, oilpriceapi.com, ECB, Eurostat)

TTF holding EUR 49.95/MWh — at the EUR 50 ceiling. Range 48.74-51.74. Qatar LNG offline persists. South Pars #2 strike re-prices Qatar exposure: South Pars/North Dome shared geological structure means Qatar's $200B+ LNG expansion is now exposed to escalation precedent. While Israel struck the Iranian petrochemical plant (NOT the gas reservoir), the precedent is alarming for any Qatar reservoir hit. Cheniere LNG (US export beneficiary) +13.8% 1M / +45.3% 3M reflects the structural Qatari/Iranian gap. European Easter Monday closure of equity markets reduced market noise — TTF will move materially Wednesday once deadline outcome is known.

EVENING UPDATE: TTF EUR 52.21/MWh (+4.2% 1D, +47% YoY). Qatar LNG disruption + deadline anxiety. EU storage at 28% — five-year seasonal low. BASF price hikes +30% from war-driven feedstock costs, Deutsche Bank upgraded to Buy. TTF directly feeding fertilizer production costs (urea via Haber-Bosch process). Rate hike speculation emerging in Europe first time since 2023. (Sources: ICE Endex, TradingEconomics, Deutsche Bank)

EUROPEAN TTF GAS LAGGING THE CEASEFIRE RELIEF — EUR49.95-50.40/MWh [REPORTED]. Pre-ceasefire TTF was at EUR52.21/MWh (April 7 evening brief). Wednesday morning data shows EUR49.95-50.40/MWh — minor decline so far. The market had a brief decline earlier this week on ceasefire optimism, then reversed when Trump escalated. Now the actual ceasefire is in place but European TTF has not yet repriced fully — likely due to (a) European trading day not yet open, (b) cautious wait for confirmed Qatar Ras Laffan restart timeline, (c) reinsurance restoration lag preventing immediate Western LNG carrier transits via Hormuz. EU storage at 28% (5-year seasonal low) means European market is still vulnerable. If ceasefire holds and Ras Laffan restart begins, TTF could fall toward EUR40-45 within 2 weeks. Wed European session is the test.

TTF ROUND-TRIP CONFIRMS HORMUZ KEY [CONFIRMED]. European TTF gas crashed -20% to EUR42.50/MWh on initial ceasefire reaction (hopes of Qatar Ras Laffan restart + Hormuz reopening lifting LNG carrier transit risk), then REBOUNDED back near EUR49.95/MWh as Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon. The intraday round-trip is the cleanest European market signal that physical gas traders did NOT believe the ceasefire framework would deliver Qatar LNG flows. TTF is now keyed to Hormuz status (operational), not headline ceasefire announcements (political). EU storage at 28% — 5-year seasonal low — means structural vulnerability persists. Cheniere LNG -3.2% as US LNG export economics weakened on TTF round-trip.

Day 44. TTF EASED TO EUR44.46/MWh [CONFIRMED]. European TTF gas down from EUR49.95 to EUR44.46/MWh (approximately -11% over two sessions), confirming the April 9 round-trip was a temporary rebound and physical market is now pricing ceasefire durability with Ras Laffan restart expectation. Still +43% above pre-war EUR31.20. Decoupling from US gas confirmed: Henry Hub at $2.65 (-0.6%) — US domestic disconnected from global crisis. Heating oil -5.0% on seasonal demand + nat gas substitution reinforces the distillate-substitution pathway. EU storage at 30% — five-year seasonal low — means summer refill vulnerability window (May-October) remains the structural risk regardless of ceasefire durability. IMPORTANT DELAY: Ras Laffan recovery timeline now August 2026 (not May 2026 as previously reported). This gives TTF a clear path lower if the ceasefire holds through summer but creates persistent vulnerability if the August restart slips further. Cheniere LNG (US export) barely moved (-0.1%) as the TTF spread narrows. For ECB: US CPI core benign at 2.6% + TTF easing = rate hike pressure reducing; stagflation trap persists but with reduced intensity through summer. ECB rate hike probability should compress materially from 76-84% over the next 1-2 weeks if TTF continues easing. TTF trigger 'reaches EUR100/MWh' remains far away at EUR44.46 — 2.25x needed. (Sources: oilpriceapi.com, ICE Endex, TradingEconomics, markets.md G)

TTF SPIKED +7.1% to ~EUR47.14/MWh on April 13 blockade day — reversed the ceasefire-driven easing. US naval blockade of Iranian ports re-raised Hormuz transit risk and LNG supply concerns. Goldman raised H1 forecast. European storage at 30% (5-year low) heading into summer refill season (May 1 start) — concerning but NOT yet crisis-level. No European energy emergency measures enacted (absence signal E5 from intel.md). EVENING DETAIL: EU gas storage at 28.8% full (net injection 614 GWh/d over April 1-7) — lowest entering gas summer since 2022. EU mandate requires 90% by October-December. Germany 30.2%, France 29%, Netherlands 23.5% — all below historical norms. Summer refill season begins May 1. With Ras Laffan offline since early March, Europe's primary concern: whether alternative LNG supplies (US, Australia, Algeria) can fill gap before winter. Goldman raised H1 TTF forecast. If TTF spikes to EUR 55-60+ on blockade escalation, triggers energy emergency measures that ripple into fertilizer costs (gas→ammonia→urea→India food security). The TTF-Hormuz linkage confirmed again: TTF responds to operational Hormuz status, not political headlines. The blockade adds a new layer of uncertainty: even with ceasefire, US naval operations in the Strait create de facto transit disruption via insurance suspension (major P&I clubs suspended Gulf coverage). Container freight (FBX Asia-USWC) at ~$2,400/FEU (+40% since pre-war) reflects broader shipping cost inflation. BASF +18.0% 1M despite TTF volatility — European chemical sector decoupling from daily TTF moves as restructuring/pricing power kicks in. The EUR100/MWh trigger remains distant at EUR47.14 (needs to more than double). (Sources: markets.md Section A & F, ICE Endex via OilPriceAPI, Goldman Sachs, ABN AMRO, TradingEconomics, Euronews, OilPriceAPI)

TTF CRASHED -7.5% to €42.95/MWh (April 14) — LARGEST SINGLE-DAY DECLINE of the entire crisis. April 15 trading €42.5-44.5. Driven by: (1) deal hopes removing LNG supply risk premium, (2) Asian LNG imports at 6-year low reducing competition for European supply, (3) milder weather suppressing heating demand. If Hormuz reopens, TTF could drop below €35 (pre-crisis ~€30). If talks fail, snap-back to €50+ immediate. Natural gas (Henry Hub) -14.2% monthly — US domestic supply completely unaffected by Gulf crisis. HH-TTF decoupling persists. (Sources: ICE Endex/TradingEconomics, market data)

EVENING UPDATE: TTF EUR42.95/MWh — LARGEST single-day decline of crisis confirmed. Three drivers: (1) Asian LNG imports at 6-year low reducing European competition, (2) mild European weather suppressing heating demand, (3) deal hopes easing Hormuz LNG transit concerns. ~55% crisis-linked, ~45% seasonal. Good for European industry: BASF +11.1% monthly on TTF crash. If Hormuz reopens, TTF could drop below EUR35 (pre-crisis ~EUR30). Container freight still +40% pre-war. (Sources: TradingEconomics/ICE, BASF data, Fortune)

TTF DRIFT LOWER CONTINUES: €41.59/MWh (-1.96% 1D, -23.91% 1M) [CONFIRMED TradingEconomics/ICE Endex]. TradingEconomics attributed decline to: (a) prospects of US-Iran ceasefire extension beyond April 22, (b) reduced Asian LNG competition (China cutting demand), (c) continued mild European weather. Technically flat day-over-day (€41.62 → €41.59) but the downward drift is intact and structural. Cheniere Energy LNG +2.3% Thursday close on structural US LNG gap-fill thesis (Qatar Ras Laffan 12.8 Mtpa offline, August earliest partial restart, 3-5 years full). Net effect for ECB: Lagarde has further cover for a hold at April 30 meeting (NOT April 17 — morning brief's 'ECB meets today' was imprecise). European storage still ~30% (5-year seasonal low). If Iran talks fail and Qatar stays offline, snap-back to €50+ immediate. (Sources: TradingEconomics April 17 TTF print, OilPriceAPI, CNBC April 16 on ECB)

Day 52 Sunday. TTF €41.25/MWh Friday settle (TradingEconomics/OilPriceAPI), -1.96% 1D, -26% month from €56 April 13 peak. Sunday SNSC institutional ratification of IRGC Hormuz closure + re-imposed $1/bbl loaded-tanker fees + confirmed Ras Laffan 3-5yr full recovery (QatarEnergy CEO Al-Kaabi March 20 reiteration; North partial within one month, South late summer per The National/OilPrice April 9) = structural floor likely re-establishes €45-50 Monday on SNSC re-pricing + kinetic broadening (container ship + cruise-liner VHF threat). Deutsche Welle running 'Europe gas security' frame on Ras Laffan partial-restart timelines — German industrial press most alarmist European voice, consistent with Germany's structural energy vulnerability. FT/Economist restrained Sunday = editorial catch-up ahead if Monday gaps hard. Storage ~30% (5-year seasonal low) keeps summer-refill vulnerability structural. If Iran re-closure event fires Monday-Tuesday, TTF snap-back to €48-52 within a week plausible. (Sources: TradingEconomics, OilPriceAPI, Deutsche Welle, markets.md Section B, intel.md B2)

MONDAY OPEN AGAINST SNSC + RAS LAFFAN 3-5YR + AL JABER HARDENING; STRUCTURAL FLOOR EUR45-50 LIKELY [REPORTED]. TTF Friday close EUR41.25/MWh (-1.96% 1D; -26% 1M; peaked EUR56 April 13). No Sunday cash print (closed). Monday opens against three Sunday-evening structural pressures: (1) SNSC ratification re-imposes $1/bbl Iranian fee + 'transit certificates' regime; (2) Ras Laffan partial restart North <1 month, South late summer, Trains 4/6 3-5 years (QatarEnergy CEO Al-Kaabi); (3) UAE ADNOC Al Jaber Sunday public hardening on chokepoint rights. Structural floor likely re-establishes EUR45-50 Monday. If cruise/container incidents continue (Mein Schiff 4 was 1; second triggers IMO-level), EUR55+. Cheniere LNG -4.4% Friday on TTF-JKM compression on ceasefire optimism = mispriced; TTF re-widening Monday lifts Cheniere. German industrial press most alarmist European voice; DAX gap-down Monday partly on TTF, not just crisis.

price_snapshots.note: EUR 47.51/MWh (-6.4% 1D) — exceeds 3% threshold, de-escalation driven pullback
~EUR47.14/MWh (+7.1% 1D) — exceeds 3% threshold, blockade-day spike reversing ceasefire easing

price_snapshots.trigger: Largest single-day decline (-7.5%) of the entire crisis
price_snapshots.delta_context: Crashed from ~€47 on deal hopes. Largest 1D move of entire conflict for this asset.

edges.context: Qatar is the world's largest LNG exporter. Ras Laffan damage (recovery delayed to August 2026) and Hormuz disruption (US blockade + war risk insurance at prohibitive prices) directly reduce European gas supply.
TTF is the benchmark for European LNG contracts. TTF spiked +7.1% on blockade day while Henry Hub flat — decoupling confirmed. HH-TTF spread is the structural arbitrage Cheniere captures.

Natural gas is the primary feedstock for urea/fertilizer production via Haber-Bosch process. TTF spike -> fertilizer production costs rise -> food security cascade. BASF +18.0% 1M on pricing power from cost pass-through.

TTF directly feeds ECB inflation — energy component swung from -3.1% to +4.9% in one month (8 pp reversal). Gas costs cascading through European industrial economy. No European energy emergency measures yet despite 30% storage.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would trigger European emergency energy protocols, industrial demand destruction, and fertilizer production shutdowns. Currently at ~EUR47.14 — needs to more than double. No European energy emergency measures yet (absence signal E5 from intel.md).
